summary:
The available evidence indicates significant oral health disparities linked to socioeconomic disadvantage, which may reflect underlying differences in dental health among recruit cohorts recruited from varied social backgrounds. These structural inequities support the likelihood of a differential in dental health despite lacking explicit military-specific data.  

\boxed{A}
